new_classes.definition_en: Spirituous beverages produced exclusively by distillation of fermented mash of plums, pears or cherries, classified under CN subheadings 2208 90 33 (containers of 2 litres or less) and 2208 90 38 (containers of more than 2 litres) in Chapter 22.

new_classes.label_en: Plum, Pear and Cherry Fermentation and Distillation Process
new_classes.label_de: Vergärung und Destillation von Pflaumen-, Birnen- oder Kirschmaische
new_classes.definition_en: Process of fermenting and distilling mash made exclusively from plums, pears or cherries to produce the corresponding fruit brandy; the sole admissible production process for CN 2208 90 33 and 2208 90 38.

new_classes.label_en: Plum, Pear and Cherry Brandy in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22089033)
new_classes.label_de: Pflaumen-, Birnen- und Kirschbranntwein in Behältnissen bis 2 Liter (KN 22089033)
new_classes.definition_en: Plum, pear or cherry brandy presented in containers with a nominal volume of 2 litres or less, classified under CN subheading 2208 90 33 (Chapter 22).

new_properties.label_en: has fruit fermentation and distillation process

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three core distinguishing criteria for CN 22089033: (1) the product type — plum, pear or cherry brandy as a Spirit subclass; (2) the exclusive production process — fermentation and distillation of the respective fruit mash only; (3) the container-size criterion — containers of 2 litres or less. The sibling code 22089038 (containers of more than 2 litres) is acknowledged via the parent class PrunePearCherryBrandy. A minor gap is that the legal note references additional definitions of 'plums' and 'cherries' via a cross-reference (indicated by 'siehe die E…' which is truncated), so any supplementary definitional nuances for those fruit terms could not be fully captured without the complete note text. Coverage is therefore set at 0.85 rather than 1.0.
